titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William H. Coon Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Janet Elaine Gertz

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday February 28, 2026 at 03:02

did.abstract: Correspondence, legal briefs, and newspaper clippings, relating to the legal case of Carrie Blueye, in Genesee County, New York and the legal case of Alice Gertrude Sharon of Batavia, New York.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: September 2025 Acquisition purchased from Tomberg Rare Books on the Hall Parker McCullough Fund.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William H. Coon was born in Saratoga Springs, New York, on December 22, 1875. He graduated from Batavia High School and later attended Cornell University and Hobart College styudying law and crime solution and prosecution.

Coon enlisted in the United States Army in 1896 and served in the Spanish American War. He was a Trustee of the Village of Batavia, N.Y., 1902-1903; Justice of the Peace of the Town of Batavia, 1904-1907; District Attorney of the County of Genesee, N.Y.,1908-1916; City historian of the City of Batavia, 1919-); City attorney of the City of Batavia; and under the name Od's krot Djoagh (O-Dan-Ghat), he served as New York State Attorney for the Tonawanda Nation of the Seneca Indians, 1908-1916 and 1928-1939.
William H. Coon died on November 14, 1953 in Batavia, New York.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William H. Coon Papers (MS 969). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These papers pertain to a lawsuit brought in 1951 by chiefs of the Seneca Nation living on the Tonawanda Indian Reservation in New York State to remove Mrs. Carrie Blueye from the reservation as an intruder, since her mother was Tuscarora and not Seneca. They are the papers of William H. Coon, a retired attorney and former district attorney of Genesee County. Coon was acting as an adviser to the current district attorney, Wallace J. Stakel, who was the prosecuting attorney in the case. Included is correspondence exchanged between Coon and Stakel providing background information on the case, memoranda concerning suggestions for the district attorney's brief, trial memoranda and briefs, and newspaper clippings about the case.
The September 2025 Acquisition contains papers relating to court cases against Alice Gertrude Sharon for providing abortions.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Suggestions for the district attorney's brief

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains files related to court cases against Alice Gertrude Sharon for performing abortions.

did.unittitle: The People of the State of New York against Alice Gertrude Sharon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The files contain records relating to court cases against Alice Gertrude Sharon for performing abortions. The subseries title is derived from the original file label that is present in the collection.

Original file label includes the text: "State of New York, Supreme Court, County of Genesee. The People of the State of New York against Alice Gertrude Sharon. Criminal manslaughter and abortion indictment. Convicted of the crime of abortion. William H. Coon, District Attorney of Genesee County, New York, Batavia, N. Y."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains accounts and an invoice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains agreements between William H. Coon and expert witnesses.

did.unittitle: Approval of County Judge in employment of Bayard J. Stedman, as counsel to Distract Attorney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a docketed court document.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a blank form.

did.unittitle: Clapham Maternity Hospital (under medical women) Jeffreys Road, S. W. Twenty-fourth annual report

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains an annual report.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a manuscript draft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains correspondence related to the court cases.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a newspaper with an article reporting that Alice G. L. Sharon had a prior arrest in Detroit, Michigan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains the original folder label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains grand jury minutes for the November 1913 term.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains indictment numbers 248-254 for different charges.

did.unittitle: Information and deposition for warrant of arrest and for search warrant

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a docketed court document.

did.unittitle: Memorandum of medical authorities. Air embolism. Entrance of air into veins, and its treatment
Metropolitan Police. Criminal Investigation Department. New Scotland Yard. Central Officers special report

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains statements about references used in court.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a docketed court document.

did.unittitle: Sharon, Alice L. G.. : business cards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains two business cards, one of which has manuscript notes on verso.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a blank piece of stationery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a pamphlet with manuscript note on title page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The subseries contains witness statements made to William H. Coon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains two copies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains subpoenas for witnesses to appear before the Grand Jury.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a docketed court document.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains arrest warrants for Alice G. Sharon.
